title: alcea pallida subsp. cretica
intro: Alcea pallida subsp. cretica, also known as the Cretan hollyhock or mountain hollyhock, is a subspecies of plant in the mallow family that is native to the mountains of Crete. The roots and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems, due to their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Alcea pallida subsp. cretica include reducing pain and inflammation, treating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Alcea pallida subsp. cretica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal use, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for therapeutic purposes.
